_comment: Sample tracker.get_issue response payload used by test-validate-story-spec-split.js. Mimics Linear/GitHub/GitLab schema-normalized output (id, identifier, description markdown, updatedAt ISO 8601, url).
title: Fixture: bifurcation valid story
description:
## Definition of Done (product)

This story is "Done" when:

1. Fixture demonstrates the canonical bifurcation business surface in the tracker description.
2. The local file contains the same heading set as mirrors with the marker.

## Problem

The split validator must observe a complete tracker description with all canonical business sections present.

## Proposed Solution

Provide this fixture as a stable input for unit and integration testing of `--split`.

## Scope

### Included

- All business sections in the tracker description.
- Tracker URL exposed.

### Excluded

Nothing technical.

## Out of Scope

| # | Non-goal | Why excluded |
|---|----------|--------------|
| OOS-1 | Tracker writes from this fixture | Fixture is read-only |
| OOS-2 | Real Linear API calls | Offline test fixture |

## Business Context

### User Journey

A developer uses /bmad-create-story on a Linear project — the workflow writes business sections here and creates a local technical file.

### Business Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **BAC-1**: Given a Linear project with spec_split_enabled=true, when the developer runs /bmad-create-story, then this fixture's content is what the tracker description looks like.
- [ ] **BAC-2**: Given this fixture, when the validator reads the tracker description, then it sees all 7 canonical business sections.

### External Dependencies

None.

## Validation Metier

- [ ] VM-1 [api] *(BAC-1)*: Tracker description contains DoD, Problem, Solution, Scope, OOS, Business Context, VM sections.
- [ ] VM-2 [api] *(BAC-2)*: Validator extracts and reports them as PRESENT.

---
↗ Technical spec: [`spec-split-local.md`](file:///fixtures/spec-split-local.md)
